title: Regression in parametrized hier blocks
body:
@gezalore 
The commit https://github.com/verilator/verilator/commit/9dc0cf5a7cd82851039c68ba310a9d822898f7b6 introduced a regression leading to a verilation failure.

```verilog
module sub;
  parametrized #(
    .ARG(1)
  ) parametrized1 (
  );
  parametrized #(
    .ARG(2)
  ) parametrized2 (
  );
endmodule

module parametrized #(
   parameter ARG=0
);
endmodule

module t;
  sub sub();
endmodule

`verilator_config
hier_block -module "sub"
```

error message:

```
verilator -f obj_vlt/t_hier_bug/Vsub__hierMkArgs.f
%Error: Module name 'ARG' is duplicated in --hierarchical-block
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.041 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

`Vsub__hierMkArgs.f` :

```
--cc
-Mdir obj_vlt/t_hier_bug/Vsub 
t/t_hier_bug.v
 --prefix Vsub
 --mod-prefix Vsub
 --top-module sub
 --lib-create sub
 --hierarchical-child 1
-GARG=\'sh1
-GARG=\'sh2
--hierarchical-block sub,sub,ARG,\'sh1,ARG,\'sh2
"--fdedup" "--debug-check" "--comp-limit-members" "10" "--x-assign" "unique" "-Wno-IMPLICIT" "+define+TEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_vlt/t_hier_bug" "+define+TEST_DUMPFILE=obj_vlt/t_hier_bug/simx.vcd"
```

 It seems that hierarchical blocks are not correctly de-parametrized leading to amalgamated configs.

Tested on latest master as of https://github.com/verilator/verilator/commit/ffd7ec10071c2b956d9237ff81e082d30d4d0511.
